name: Pat Flowers
abstract: Ivelee Patrick Flowers (October 16, 1917 – October 6, 2000) was an American jazz pianist and singer. Born in Detroit, Flowers began his professional career as the pianist during intermissions at Uncle Tom's Cabin in the city when he was 18 years old.

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been overseas another time with Slam Stewart, Johnny Guarnieri, Pat Flowers and we did a tribute to Fats, it was called a “Tribute to Fats Waller.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
